verifier_issues.detail: The .xlsx artifact preview parse_status is 'openpyxl_unavailable' with error 'No module named \u0027openpyxl\u0027'. This prevents automated verification that the workbook cells contain formulas (vs. static values) and that per-record expert-review flags are implemented in-sheet.
verifier_issues.summary: Workbook file could not be programmatically parsed

verifier_issues.detail: The grader's semantic score (1.0) is based on the monitoring_plan.md narrative and reproduced numeric tables. Those previews reproduce row-level calculations and remediation combinatorics consistently, supporting the grader's claim, but absence of a parsed .xlsx means workbook-level execution/formula presence is unverified. Manual inspection of the workbook is recommended before full trust.
verifier_issues.summary: Semantic correctness claims rely on artifact text preview

The primary limitation is that the .xlsx could not be parsed programmatically (openpyxl unavailable), so cell-level verification of computed numeric values and in-sheet row-level citations could not be confirmed automatically.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

passed_checks.name: evidence_presence
passed_checks.notes: Both required artifacts and monitoring_plan preview are present and referenced by grader.

passed_checks.name: evidence_alignment
passed_checks.notes: Monitoring plan preview and agent trace both document the assumed weights and remediation options cited by the grader.

passed_checks.name: score_rationale
passed_checks.notes: Model's zero semantic score is justified by documented imputation of weights; deterministic precheck score pertains to artifact contract only.

passed_checks.name: no_hidden_leak
passed_checks.notes: hidden_reference_leaked and hidden_reference_used flags are false in grader outputs.

passed_checks.name: cell_level_verification
passed_checks.notes: Could not programmatically open .xlsx (openpyxl unavailable). Verifier recommends manual spreadsheet inspection to fully confirm computed cells and in-sheet row-level citations.

recommended_actions: Manually open deviation_impact_rank.xlsx and confirm the workbook cells reproduce the numeric values claimed in the monitoring_plan and actual_outputs, and that every derived cell includes an in-sheet row-level citation to the embedded packet.
If the assumed weights are actually present in the embedded packet or workbook (but not visible in text previews), rescind the semantic penalty and re-evaluate numeric classifications.
If weights are not present, ensure the worker documents 'needs_review' flags and does not compute final authoritative numeric classifications using invented parameters; require rework per rubric.
